name: Microsoft/ms-hard-delete-purge-history
description: Hard delete and $purge-history conformance tests (from DeleteTests, split out of CRUD/delete.json): both are extended capabilities not mandated by base FHIR R4, so all assertions are warningOnly. DELETE ?hardDelete=true permanently erases a resource and all version history; $purge-history removes prior versions while keeping the current version accessible.

variable.name: hardDeleteId
variable.description: server-assigned id of the Patient used for the hard-delete test

variable.name: hardDeleteVersion
variable.description: initial versionId of the hard-delete Patient, used to confirm it is no longer vread-able after a hard delete

variable.name: purgeId
variable.description: server-assigned id of the Patient used for the $purge-history test

variable.name: purgeVersion1
variable.description: first versionId of the purge-history Patient, expected to be purged

variable.name: purgeVersion2
variable.description: second (current) versionId of the purge-history Patient, expected to remain accessible after $purge-history

test.name: hard delete permanently removes the resource and all version history
test.description: Extended capability (not mandated by base FHIR R4): DELETE Patient/{id}?hardDelete=true after a normal (soft) delete must permanently erase the resource so that a plain read, a vread of its original version, and its instance history all return 404 Not Found. All assertions are warningOnly since hard delete is a server extension not every conformant server implements.

operation.description: Create the Patient that will be soft- then hard-deleted

assert.description: Soft delete must return a 2xx success status
Server extension: expect a 2xx success status when hard delete is supported

operation.description: Attempt to read the hard-deleted Patient

assert.description: Server extension: expect 404 Not Found for a hard-deleted resource

operation.description: Attempt to vread the hard-deleted Patient's original version

assert.description: Server extension: expect 404 Not Found for a purged version

operation.description: Attempt to fetch instance history for the hard-deleted Patient

assert.description: Server extension: expect 404 Not Found for the history of a hard-deleted resource

test.name: $purge-history removes prior versions while keeping the current version accessible
test.description: Extended capability (not mandated by base FHIR R4): after updating a resource so it has two versions, POST/DELETE Patient/{id}/$purge-history must remove the prior version(s) while the current version stays fully readable. All assertions are warningOnly since $purge-history is a server extension not every conformant server implements. Gated on the target advertising Patient/$purge-history, either as a resource-scoped or a system-level operation.

operation.description: Create the Patient that will accumulate history and then be purged
Read back the Patient to use as the round-tripped update body
Round-trip update the Patient to produce a second version

assert.description: Update must return a 2xx success status

operation.description: Purge prior version history for the Patient (server extension operation)

assert.description: Server extension: expect a 2xx success status when $purge-history is supported

operation.description: Read the current version after purging prior history

assert.description: Server extension: current version must still be readable after purge
Server extension: current version must remain the latest versionId

operation.description: Attempt to vread the purged prior version

assert.description: Server extension: primary expectation is 404 Not Found for a purged version
Server extension: alternative expectation is 410 Gone for a purged version

operation.description: Teardown: delete the hard-delete Patient (already hard-deleted by the test if supported, best effort)
